title: Storyboard · "Knock" — Radio→Animation production script · S12
body:
**Scene 12: 14:30–16:00 · The Library**

HERO FRAME: Interior of a small American public library, 1949 · oak shelves, green-shaded reading lamps, a single chair at a long oak table · an open book at the chair · a Bible visible on the table alongside it · stained glass over the entrance casting colored light on the floor

MOTION: kling — the colored light from the stained glass slowly shifts across the floor as if from the moving sun. A book's page turns gently from an unseen draft. The reading lamp on the table flickers once.

MOOD: Old quiet wisdom held in safekeeping.

AUDIO: Walter: *"I have read more in eight weeks than in my whole life before. The libraries are full. The books are still here. I have started Dickens. I have started Hugo. I have started the Bible."*

source.label: Dimension X episode #05 (NBC Radio, May 6, 1950) adapting Fredric Brown's 1948 short-short story "Knock" (originally in *Thrilling Wonder Stories*, December 1948)

author: matt

witnesses.label: Rufinus of Aquileia, Commentary on the Apostles' Creed (c. AD 404)
Pirminius, De singulis libris canonicis scarapsus (c. AD 750) — earliest received form
Cited by every major catechism since 8th century
